name: security-bluebook-builder
description: Build security Blue Books for sensitive apps
qualityReason: Specific document type with clear use
capabilities: Generate security documentation templates for applications
Provide guidance on security control documentation
Document security requirements and testing procedures
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs and weak cryptographic algorithms, but evaluation reveals these are false positives. The URLs are legitimate documentation links to the skill's GitHub repository. The cryptographic flag was triggered by the word 'build' in 'build security blue books', which refers to documentation creation, not cryptography. This is a simple documentation skill with no security concerns.
valueStatement: Create comprehensive security documentation for sensitive applications. This skill provides patterns and guidance for building security Blue Books that document security controls, threat models, and compliance requirements.
limitations: Does not execute security scans or tests
Does not generate actual code or implementation
Does not provide real-time security monitoring
Requires human review for accuracy and completeness

useCases.targetUser: Security engineers and architects documenting new applications
useCases.title: New Application Security Documentation
useCases.description: Generate initial security Blue Book structure for a new sensitive application, including security controls, threat model sections, and compliance mappings.

useCases.title: Compliance Documentation Update
useCases.description: Refresh existing security documentation to meet new compliance requirements or update threat model sections.

useCases.title: Security Documentation Review
useCases.description: Review and improve existing security Blue Books for completeness and accuracy.

promptTemplates.title: Create New Blue Book
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a security Blue Book for my new application [application name]. It handles [sensitive data types] and is deployed on [infrastructure]. Generate a comprehensive documentation structure including threat model, security controls, and compliance sections.

promptTemplates.title: Add Threat Model Section
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a threat model section to the existing security Blue Book for [application]. Include sections for assets, threat actors, attack vectors, and mitigation strategies.
promptTemplates.scenario: Expanding existing documentation with threat modeling

promptTemplates.title: Map Compliance Requirements
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a compliance mapping section for [regulation/standard] in our security Blue Book. Map our existing security controls to the required compliance criteria.

promptTemplates.title: Review Security Controls
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the security controls section of our Blue Book for [application]. Identify gaps, suggest improvements, and ensure coverage of OWASP Top 10 and common attack vectors.

bestPractices: Start with a clear data classification section to define what needs protection
Include both technical controls and procedural security measures
Regularly update threat models as the application evolves
antiPatterns: Copy-pasting generic security templates without customization
Ignoring non-functional security requirements like incident response
Failing to document security decisions and their rationale

faq.question: What is a security Blue Book?
faq.answer: A security Blue Book is comprehensive documentation that outlines an application's security controls, threat models, compliance requirements, and security architecture decisions.

faq.question: Does this skill perform security testing?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates documentation templates and guidance. It does not execute security scans or tests.

faq.question: Which compliance standards does this support?
faq.answer: The skill can help document compliance with various standards including SOC 2, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, and ISO 27001.

faq.question: Can I use this for cloud infrastructure documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides guidance for documenting security controls for cloud-based applications and infrastructure.

faq.question: How detailed is the generated documentation?
faq.answer: The skill generates comprehensive outlines and templates. You should expand each section with specific details about your application.

faq.question: Does this work for microservices architectures?
faq.answer: Yes, the documentation templates can be adapted for microservices, including service-to-service security and API security sections.
